data.title: ARK Invest Highlights Bitcoin's Strong Fundamentals Amid Institutional Adoption
data.description:
According to PANews, ARK Invest's latest "Bitcoin Quarterly" report emphasizes the robust fundamentals of Bitcoin, noting strong potential demand as indicated by network activity, profitability, and supply distribution. The report highlights that long-term holders show no signs of selling, and on-chain metrics remain bullish. Most tokens are profitable and held by investors with low spending tendencies, providing favorable support for Bitcoin's price in the fourth quarter. Medium-sized investors continue to accumulate, while large holders have slowed their selling, contributing to a healthier upward trend.

Institutional adoption of Bitcoin is accelerating, with digital asset trusts and spot Bitcoin ETFs collectively holding approximately 12.2% of the total supply. This underscores Bitcoin's deepening integration with traditional markets, offering stable demand and increasing its potential as a strategic asset allocation. On the macroeconomic front, controlled inflation and a weak labor market are prompting shifts in Federal Reserve policies, alongside government measures that may drive "productivity-driven economic growth," reinforcing bullish signals.

However, ARK Invest cautions that greater volatility may emerge later in 2025, but the fundamentals continue to create favorable conditions for Bitcoin's potential rise.

data.title: Market Faces Severe Losses Amid Liquidation Crisis
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua has highlighted the significant impact of recent market liquidations, amounting to hundreds of billions of dollars. The primary affected groups are market makers and active traders, with the damage to the market exceeding expectations. Yi emphasized the need for extended recovery of investment confidence and liquidity, particularly for altcoins that have experienced drastic devaluation.

Yi strongly recommends that trading platforms allocate a portion of their profits to establish liquidity regulation funds to prevent liquidity exhaustion in extreme situations. He noted that the Federal Reserve has market regulation capabilities, and platforms with annual profits in the billions of dollars have both the capacity and responsibility to implement such mechanisms. Without these measures, the market could suffer devastating damage, affecting not only platform users but also the market and the trading platforms themselves.

data.title: Imperial Bank of Commerce: Gold will rise to $4,500 in the next two years
data.description: In her latest forecast, Anita Soni, an analyst at Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce Capital Markets, predicts that gold prices will rise to $4,500 per ounce in 2026 and 2027, before falling back to $4,250 in 2028 and $4,000 in 2029. The analyst stated that she still expects gold to face a positive macroeconomic environment. Uncertainty regarding tariffs will persist, and the negative impact of tariffs implemented to date and those expected to be implemented on consumer purchasing power has yet to fully manifest in the US economy. Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve succumbed to Trump's calls for rate cuts earlier than Soni expected. Soni believes that while the rise in gold prices earlier this year was related to rate cuts, the recent parabolic surge stems from concerns about long-term inflation and wealth preservation, as the Fed's monetary policy has not specifically focused on long-term inflation. (Gold Ten)

data.title: He Yi: Binance will handle user losses caused by platform issues, excluding personal trading losses caused by price fluctuations
data.description: Binance co-founder He Yi stated in a post on the X platform that Binance will address losses and margin calls incurred by traders during the platform downtime and will gradually address issues affecting users of the three types of unpegged and wrapped assets. Binance will not recover profits from users who purchased unpegged assets at low prices. He Yi reiterated that Binance does not compensate individual trading losses caused by price fluctuations or unrealized profits.

data.title: BitMine's ETH holdings have a floating loss of $1.95 billion
data.description: According to Solid Intel, BitMine currently holds 2.65 million ETH, with an average purchase cost of approximately $4,200. Based on the current price of $3,300, the value of its portfolio has dropped to approximately $8.7 billion, leaving it facing a $1.95 billion unrealized loss.

data.description: According to Scam Sniffer | Web 3 Anti-Scam, a user lost $72,572 worth of ASTER tokens by signing a malicious "permit" signature.

data.title: Gold Prices Projected to Rise Amid Economic Uncertainty
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce Capital Markets analyst Anita Soni forecasts that gold prices will increase to $4,500 per ounce in 2026 and 2027, before declining to $4,250 in 2028 and $4,000 in 2029. Soni anticipates a favorable macroeconomic environment for gold, despite ongoing uncertainty in tariff policies. The negative impact of tariffs on consumer purchasing power has not yet fully manifested in the U.S. economy.

Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve has responded to U.S. President Donald Trump's calls for interest rate cuts sooner than Soni expected. She attributes the earlier rise in gold prices to these rate cuts, while the recent parabolic surge is driven by concerns over long-term inflation and wealth preservation, as the Fed's monetary policy does not specifically address long-term inflation.

data.title: Bitmine increased its holdings by 27,256 ETH in the past hour, worth $104 million
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, Bitmine purchased another 27,256 ETH worth US$104 million in the past hour.

data.title: Binance CEO: We apologize to all affected users and will learn lessons and properly handle user issues
data.description: Richard Teng, CEO of Binance, posted on the X platform: "The cryptocurrency market has been volatile over the past 24 hours, and I know many of our users have encountered challenges. I would like to offer my sincere apologies to all affected users. We will not make excuses - we will listen carefully, learn from this incident, and strive to do better. If you still have any unresolved issues, please contact the Binance Support team. Every case will be treated with due attention and appropriate handling, and compensation will be provided where applicable. Market volatility is normal, and we encourage all users to stay informed and operate with caution during this period. Thank you for your continued support."

data.title: GSR says its OTC trading desk remains extremely active and able to handle client requests

data.description:
Decentralized finance (DeFi) leaders are hailing the resilience of blockchain-based trading and lending protocols after one of the largest market sell-offs of the year, triggered by renewed trade tensions between the United States and China. The turbulence came after President Donald Trump announced that starting November 1, he will impose new 100% tariffs on Chinese […]

data.title: Over 6,300 wallets on Hyperliquid lost over $1.23 billion during the market downturn
data.description: According to Lookonchain, over 1,000 wallets on Hyperliquid were completely liquidated during the market downturn. This left over 6,300 wallets in the red, collectively holding over $1.23 billion. Of these, 205 wallets lost over $1 million, and over 1,070 lost over $100,000.
